WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $92.57 (+4.1%) vs history prev_close $96.21 (+0.1%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Wednesday's session snapped the two-day relief rally hard — SPY fell 1.31% to $661.43 and QQQ dropped 1.37% to $594.90, with VIX surging 12.16% to 25.09, erasing the calm that briefly returned after Monday's bounce. The FOMC held rates at 3.50–3.75% as expected; per US Bank, the Fed's updated Summary of Economic Projections raised its headline PCE inflation forecast to 2.7% and core PCE to 2.7% for 2026, while still pencilling in one 25-basis-point cut this year — a hawkish-leaning hold that reinforced the rate-pause narrative and weighed on tech multiples. Micron delivered blowout fiscal Q2 results with record revenue, but per Investor's Business Daily, MU shares skidded post-earnings as the market processed cautious macro signals from Fed Chair Powell alongside the print.
commodities_note: WTI crude shows a data conflict: the pipeline's fast_info feed reports $96.35 (+4.08% daily) while the historical close shows $96.21 (+0.1%) — the +4.08% daily figure is used as the headline move but treat intraday precision with caution given the conflict; the 5-day trend is the more reliable signal here. The Hormuz disruption enters Day 10 with shipping traffic still collapsed near 2 vessels per day versus the 138-vessel baseline, keeping a structural supply premium in crude even as demand destruction fears cap the upside. Gold fell 2.69% to $4,858 — a notable reversal from its extended run; given gold remains significantly above any near-term support and the daily move is consistent with profit-taking after an extended run, no attractive entry in GLD exists at current levels.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Would reassess above SGD 58.50 with volume confirmation or below SGD 56.50 if NIM compression guidance worsens.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Becomes actionable on a pullback toward SGD 21.00 near its rising 50-day average, or on evidence that NIM stabilises at May earnings.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Would upgrade to WATCH on a sustained move above SGD 37.36 (50-day average) with above-average volume.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — the prior WATCH thesis depended on a near-term technical entry, but with RSI at 46.2 in neutral-bearish territory, price fractionally below the 50-day average at SGD 6.62, and only 2 sessions of SMA50 reclaim (which have already reversed), the entry gate fails on directional confirmation. Singapore Airlines is fairly valued at 17.4x forward earnings relative to the cluster median of 13.9x — a 25% premium that is partially justified by its 8.28% free cash flow yield and 5.23% dividend, though per analyst consensus, earnings are expected to decline 63% and revenue growth sits at just 1.9%, making the premium hard to sustain without an operational catalyst. The Hormuz disruption compounds the caution: higher jet fuel costs and rerouting expenses are a direct P&L headwind for an airline, and the SGD 6.34 support zone is the next meaningful downside reference. At current levels, the risk/reward does not support fresh entry — this is a stock to own for the yield only if you can stomach fuel-cost volatility. Wait for a confirmed hold above SGD 6.65 resistance with volume before reconsidering.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 59 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from WATCH to CAUTION — blocked at +6.4% above the 50-day average with RSI at 58.7, which exceeds the 55 threshold required for a trend exception. Singtel at SGD 5.10 sits 81% above the Singapore cluster median forward PE, and with revenue growth of just 0.9% against a forward PE of 25.2x and a PEG of 3.74, the valuation premium is difficult to justify on fundamentals alone. The structural driver — a telecom benefiting from a digital infrastructure cycle — is real but not yet translating into the earnings growth needed to underpin this multiple. Resistance at SGD 5.15 is the immediate ceiling; a pullback toward the SGD 4.79 50-day average (roughly -6%) would be the first zone worth reassessing. Not attractive at current levels — the premium is too high for 0.9% revenue growth.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA sits 2.54% below its declining 50-day average of $185.09 with RSI at 45.6 — technically in no-man's land between the $177.41 support and $184.47 resistance. The fundamental story remains compelling: per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78 billion implies Blackwell is shipping faster than many analysts had modelled, and per Reuters, Elon Musk confirmed SpaceX AI and Tesla will continue ordering NVDA chips at scale. Jensen Huang's statement that the company sees over $1 trillion in cumulative sales through 2027, reported by Yahoo Finance, is a bullish forward signal — but this comes directly from the company's CEO, so note the inherent seller bias in that projection. At 16.4x forward PE with a PEG of 1.1 and 73% revenue growth, NVDA is actually the cheapest it has been on a growth-adjusted basis in over a year — a 11.8% discount to the semis cluster median PE. The 200-day average at $178.05 is only 1.3% below current price and represents the next major structural support. The thesis is intact; the entry is not yet clean. Bottom line: NVDA's price has come in to where the fundamentals start looking genuinely cheap, but the 50-day average is still declining and the market just had a bad day — wait for the $177–179 zone to hold on a test with volume before entering.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD is 7.14% below its declining 50-day average of $214.80, and while today's +1.69% bounce is encouraging, it does not clear the technical barrier. The Samsung MOU announced March 18 — covering primary HBM4 supply for the MI455X GPU and advanced DDR5 for 6th-generation EPYC CPUs — meaningfully deepens AMD's memory access and opens a second foundry manufacturing relationship, per Simply Wall St. reporting. The confirmed Meta GPU supply deal (per AMD IR press release, February 24, 2026) covering up to 6GW of Instinct GPUs through 2030 validates AMD as a credible hyperscale alternative to NVDA. At 18.6x forward PE with a 0.58 PEG and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is the most compelling valuation on the watchlist — trading at exactly the cluster median PE but growing nearly twice as fast as peers. The 200-day average at $192.10 is 3.7% below current price and is the key support to watch; a break there would be structurally bearish. On the upside, the $218.84 resistance aligns with the declining 50-day average zone and will be a meaningful test when approached. Bottom line: AMD is the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to how fast it's growing, but the price trend is still pointing down — wait for the 50-day average to flatten or for AMD to hold the $192 support level with volume before entering.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 45x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel is 2.93% below its 50-day average of $46.39, but the entry block stands — the pipeline flags a soft block for forward PE of 45x, which is 144% above the semis cluster median, and with revenue shrinking 4.1% year-over-year and negative free cash flow yield of -2.0%, no earnings growth catalyst justifies the multiple today. The 18A node thesis — Intel's proprietary gate-all-around transistor technology with back-side power delivery — remains the long-term thesis, with per ASML Q4 2025 earnings the first commercial High-NA EUV tool installed at Intel targeting a H2 2026 ramp; Intel's April 24 earnings (36 days out) are the next concrete milestone for foundry progress. The $42.58 support zone is the key downside reference, 5.4% below current price, with the 200-day average at $33.94 as the deeper structural floor if that breaks. This is a turnaround story where the thesis is real but the timeline is long and the valuation demands patience. Not attractive at 45x forward earnings with shrinking revenue — revisit after April 24 earnings for 18A customer pipeline updates.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — ASML broke back below its 50-day average of $1,382.08, closing at $1,355.17 (-2.43%), which eliminates the technical entry that justified the prior WATCH signal. Per Reuters, Belgian chip research institute imec secured one of fewer than a dozen ASML High-NA EUV machines worldwide on March 18, with Intel and SK Hynix preparing to deploy High-NA tools for AI and high-bandwidth memory chips as early as 2027 — a concrete confirmation that the technology roadmap is real and customer adoption is advancing. Per Tom's Hardware, ASML projects €34–39 billion in 2026 revenue and $71 billion by 2030, driven by AI infrastructure demand. At 31.3x forward PE with a PEG of 2.33 and revenue growth of just 4.9%, the valuation is stretched relative to near-term fundamentals — even if the 2030 thesis is compelling. The $1,407.54 resistance and the declining 50-day average are the ceiling; the next support of note is the $1,008.68 zone (26% below), making this a wide-ranging name where entry discipline matters. ASML reports April 15 (27 days out) — that print will be the first real test of whether the 2026 revenue guide holds. Bottom line: ASML is the world's only EUV lithography supplier and the long-term thesis is unimpeachable, but right now it's expensive for near-term growth and the price just failed a key technical level — wait for the April 15 earnings to confirm demand, then reassess.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.3% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=8 (<30), RSI 64 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to WATCH — signal unchanged, but the thesis crystallised materially overnight. Micron reported blowout fiscal Q2 results with record revenue; per GuruFocus, the quarter included strategic agreements and record margins, while per Yahoo Finance, CFRA analyst Angelo Zino flagged that margins may be peaking — a key risk to monitor. MU CEO's projection of ~40% memory price increases through Q2 2026 is a bullish call from a seller with an obvious interest in that outcome — independent corroboration is needed before treating it as consensus. The entry block remains firmly in place: MU is 16.33% above its 50-day average of $396.92, only 8 sessions above that average (minimum 30 required for a trend exception), and RSI at 64.3 exceeds the 55 threshold. At 8.1x forward PE with a PEG of 0.64 and 56.7% revenue growth — 56.5% below the semis cluster median PE — MU is structurally cheap if the memory cycle continues. The $357.67–363.90 support zone is the first meaningful pullback target, representing a 21–23% decline from current levels. No actionable entry exists yet. Bottom line: Micron just proved the memory cycle is real with record results, but the stock has already priced in a lot of good news at 16% above its 50-day average — wait for a pullback toward the $360 zone before entering, or watch for the extension to compress naturally over the next 30 sessions.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — AMZN broke further below its declining 50-day average of $222.23, now sitting 5.56% below it at $209.87, with RSI at 45.3 and the SMA50 declining, which crosses into clear CAUTION territory. The $200 billion AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) is the central tension: AWS cloud revenue is accelerating, and the Cerebras partnership for AI inference on Amazon Bedrock (announced March 13, per Insider Monkey) adds a performance dimension, but per Motley Fool, investors are questioning when this spend translates into earnings given the Q1 operating income miss. At 22.5x forward PE with a PEG of 1.97 and 13.6% revenue growth — in line with the BigTech cluster median — AMZN is not obviously cheap. Free cash flow yield of just 1.06% means the thesis depends entirely on future earnings delivery from the capex cycle. Support at $196.93 is 6.2% below current price; a break there opens the $161.38 zone. Bottom line: Amazon's cloud business is genuinely accelerating, but a $200 billion spending plan means profits are being deferred for years — at today's price you're paying a fair multiple for a lot of uncertainty about when the investment pays off. Avoid until the 50-day average stops declining or price holds $197 support with volume.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet at $306.30 remains 3.83% below its declining 50-day average of $318.50, with RSI at 46.7 — technically weak with no entry signal. Per Reuters, DeepMind founder Demis Hassabis announced Bridgewater's chief scientist Jasjeet Sekhon is joining DeepMind as chief strategy officer, a meaningful talent acquisition for the AI unit but not a near-term price catalyst. Per Simply Wall St., the stock has returned 94% over the past year, raising the question of whether the AI multiple is already embedded. At 22.8x forward PE with a PEG of 2.33 and 18% revenue growth, GOOG is fairly valued by BigTech standards — neither cheap nor expensive in isolation, but the declining SMA50 argues against entry. The $295.95 support is 3.4% below current price and is the level to watch; the 200-day average at $258.80 is the deeper structural floor. GOOG and AMZN report within days of each other in late April — that earnings window is the next real catalyst. No entry at current levels.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +33.6% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 25% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from BUY to CAUTION — blocked at +33.6% above the 50-day average, which exceeds the 20% extreme extension threshold where no trend exception applies regardless of duration or RSI. LITE surged another 6.99% to $700.81 on Wednesday, driven by S&P 500 index fund buying ahead of its official inclusion next week, per Insider Monkey. The NVDA $2 billion strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor relations press release, March 2, 2026) and record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $665.5 million (+65.5% year-over-year) validate the long-term AI optics thesis. At a forward PE of 48.2x with a PEG of 0.15 and 65.5% revenue growth, the valuation is extreme on a PE basis but the PEG suggests growth is outrunning the multiple rapidly. Do not use the 50-day average as an entry reference — a pullback to the 50-day average from here would require a crash-level move of roughly 25%. The $528.01 shorter-term support zone is the first meaningful level to watch on any reversal. Inclusion-driven momentum is a known exhaustion pattern — per lessons from this watchlist, S&P 500 inclusion spikes typically reverse within 3–5 days post-event. Not attractive at current levels — wait for the inclusion excitement to fade and price to stabilise near $528–540 support before reconsidering.

COHR.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.7% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 55 (>=55).
COHR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — blocked at +13.67% above the 50-day average with RSI at exactly 55.0, which meets the ≥55 threshold that blocks the trend exception, eliminating the prior WATCH entry basis. COHR rose 3.42% to $257.21 on Wednesday, with Stifel raising its price target per StockStory, citing COHR's strong position in AI datacenter buildout. The NVDA $2 billion strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion are structural tailwinds, and at a forward PE of 35x with a PEG of 0.06 — the lowest PEG on the entire watchlist — growth is dramatically outpacing the multiple. The $230.10 support zone is the first meaningful pullback target, approximately 10.5% below current price. Resistance at $300.20 is the next upside target if momentum continues. The risk here is that LITE's inclusion-driven spike exhausts first and drags COHR lower in the same rotation — both names move together. Bottom line: COHR has genuinely exceptional growth relative to its valuation, but at 13.7% above its 50-day average with RSI at the boundary, the entry gate is blocked — add to the watchlist if it pulls back to the $230–240 zone.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 73 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal overridden from HOLD to CAUTION — blocked at +11.0% above the 50-day average with RSI at 72.6 (overbought zone), eliminating any entry case. The Hormuz disruption's oil price premium is real and structural so long as the conflict continues, but at RSI 72.6 and 11% above the 50-day average, XLE is technically extended by every measure. XLE has gained 8.71% over the past month as crude prices surged. The $43.08 support zone is roughly 26% below current price — the 200-day average at $45.73 is nearer but still 21.7% lower, confirming there is no clean nearby stop. As an ETF, standard PE/valuation checks do not apply. Avoid at current levels — XLE only becomes interesting on a meaningful pullback, which would likely coincide with a Hormuz de-escalation or demand destruction accelerating. If crude pulls back toward $85–88, revisit XLE near the $50–52 range (approximate 50-day average equivalent). Note: if SPY breaks below $661, XLE historically faces forced selling as investors exit risk assets broadly.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 82x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — at $152.77, PLTR is sitting essentially on its 50-day average of $152.67 (+0.07%), which technically qualifies for the entry zone, but the pipeline's soft block for forward PE of 82x requires explicit catalyst justification before escalating to WATCH. The $10 billion Army IDIQ framework ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — meaning this is a contract vehicle, not firm revenue) and the $448 million Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) represent real defense AI wins, and per Motley Fool, PLTR's $11.2 billion revenue backlog and 61% year-over-year revenue growth guidance for 2026 are structurally compelling. However, at 82x forward PE — 258% above the BigTech cluster median — and a PEG of 3.22, the valuation is pricing in near-perfect execution for years. Michael Burry's short thesis, per Benzinga, centres on PLTR being miscategorised as a defence-pure play; while his track record warrants attention, the counter-argument is the backlog and government contract visibility. RSI at 54.3 is neutral. The $119.17 support is the downside reference if macro deteriorates. Would upgrade to WATCH only if RSI remains below 55 AND price holds the 50-day average for 5+ consecutive sessions with improving volume. Not a buy at 82x forward earnings — the story is compelling but the price tag demands more evidence of earnings delivery.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above the 50-day average of $132.14 with SPY stabilising above $665.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains in uniform CAUTION/HOLD for a ninth consecutive session with no macro reset signal — US10Y at 4.26% and STI still below 5,000 mean the rate-pause thesis driving net interest margin compression is intact. The most notable development today is SIA (C6L_SI) being downgraded from WATCH to HOLD as the Hormuz-driven jet fuel cost risk makes entry unattractive at a 25% PE premium to the cluster. DBS and OCBC hold their technical positions but low volume across all five names signals conviction is absent.
singapore.key_development: SIA downgraded to HOLD as Hormuz fuel cost headwind eliminates the prior entry case at a cluster-premium valuation.

semiconductors.summary: ASML's failure to hold its 50-day average on a risk-off day triggers a downgrade from WATCH to CAUTION, completing a full CAUTION sweep of the mega-cap semis even as MU's blowout earnings validate the memory cycle thesis. The structural divergence — mid-cap memory strength (MU) versus extended mega-cap weakness (NVDA, AMD, ASML) — remains the defining feature, with the FOMC's hawkish hold adding multiple compression pressure across the board. INTC's April 24 earnings and ASML's April 15 report are the next concrete catalysts to watch for any signal changes.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Full CAUTION sweep on mega-caps; memory cycle intact but priced in
semiconductors.key_development: ASML broke back below its 50-day average on Wednesday, reversing the technical inflection that drove last session's WATCH upgrade.

big_tech.summary: All three BigTech names remain on defensive signals — AMZN downgraded to CAUTION as it crosses the 5.56% below SMA50 threshold, joining GOOG in technical breakdown territory. PLTR reverts to HOLD as it sits exactly on its 50-day average but cannot clear the 82x forward PE justification bar for a WATCH upgrade. The FOMC's hawkish hold is the direct headwind — rate-sensitive, high-multiple tech names face sustained multiple compression until rate cut expectations firm up.
big_tech.thesis_status: Defensive — declining SMAs dominate all three names
big_tech.key_development: AMZN downgraded to CAUTION as the stock crosses 5.56% below a declining 50-day average, eliminating the prior HOLD entry option.

photonics.summary: Both LITE and COHR are now on CAUTION — LITE hard-blocked at 33.6% extreme extension driven by S&P 500 pre-inclusion buying, COHR blocked at 13.67% extension with RSI hitting exactly the 55 threshold that eliminates the trend exception. The NVDA $2 billion investment in each (confirmed via NVIDIA IR press releases) and AI datacenter demand are genuinely structural, but the technical setup for both names is fully extended — the inclusion-driven momentum is a known exhaustion pattern. Wait for post-inclusion selling pressure to create a re-entry zone.
photonics.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically blocked — await inclusion exhaustion
photonics.key_development: COHR downgraded from WATCH to CAUTION as RSI hit 55 exactly, the boundary that closes the trend exception window.

energy.summary: XLE remains hard-blocked at RSI 72.6 (overbought) and 11% above its 50-day average — no entry exists despite the Hormuz premium in crude. The structural oil price support from the crisis is intact, but the ETF has already priced in a sustained disruption scenario. XLE only becomes interesting on a pullback toward the $50–52 zone.
energy.key_development: FOMC hawkish hold adds a demand-destruction risk overlay to energy — higher rates slow economic activity and reduce oil demand over 2–3 quarters.

index.summary: VUAA_L (S&P 500 ETF) at $128.48 is 2.77% below a declining 50-day average with RSI at 40.3 — the Wednesday sell-off on the FOMC decision worsened an already fragile technical picture. The SPY close at $661.43 broke below the critical $665 support range flagged in recent sessions, which is a structurally negative signal for index-level exposure. No entry until the 50-day average of $132.14 is reclaimed.
index.thesis_status: CAUTION — 50-day average declining, key support broken
index.key_development: SPY's 1.31% decline on FOMC day broke the $665 support level that had been holding since the March 9 bounce, resetting the technical picture to fresh breakdown risk.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: AMD's Samsung MOU adds a second foundry option, but TSMC remains the primary manufacturer for AMD MI-series GPUs — the Meta $100B supply deal (per AMD IR) means TSMC fabs run at elevated utilisation through 2030.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM (US ADR) pulls back 8–10% from current levels, which would likely coincide with a broader semi correction bringing it closer to its 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Directly linked to COHR and LITE — Broadcom's silicon photonics and networking ASICs sit in the same AI datacenter interconnect stack that NVDA's $2B investment in LITE and COHR is building out.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor AVGO after the LITE/COHR inclusion-driven spike exhausts — if photonics names pull back 15–20% from peak, AVGO often follows and may offer a cleaner entry into the same AI optics theme.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The primary HBM (high-bandwidth memory) supplier to NVDA's Blackwell architecture — MU's blowout Q2 earnings and the memory price cycle directly read through to SK Hynix's order book and margin trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for entry if MU pulls back toward $360 support and SK Hynix (in KRW, ~$180 USD equivalent per ADR proxy) corrects proportionally — the two names trade in close correlation on HBM pricing cycles.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz conflict choking global oil supply — Day 10, 2 vessels/day vs 138 baseline
US-China technology export controls expanding to subsidiaries of sanctioned firms
FOMC hawkish hold adding rate-pressure headwind to risk assets globally
geopolitical.macro_outlook: The FOMC's decision to hold rates at 3.50–3.75% while raising its PCE inflation forecasts to 2.7% confirms the rate-pause narrative is entrenched for at least another quarter, directly pressuring high-multiple tech names on the watchlist. The Hormuz crisis enters its tenth day with no diplomatic resolution visible — per ABC News, Iran's new supreme leader has framed the closure as a sustained pressure tool, not a negotiating chip. These two forces compound each other: oil above $96 pushes inflation forecasts higher, which pushes rate cuts further out, which pressures tech multiples further.

base_case.description: Hormuz remains semi-blocked at 2–10 vessels per day with Iran controlling a de facto safe corridor for select traffic; WTI holds $90–100 range; Fed delivers one 25bp cut in H2 2026; US-China tech restrictions expand incrementally but stop short of full semiconductor decoupling.
base_case.market_impact: SPY stabilises in the $650–670 range; energy names remain elevated but overbought; semis trade sideways with stock-specific divergence (MU strong, NVDA/AMD range-bound); Singapore financials stay under pressure from persistent high rates.
base_case.portfolio_action: Hold current defensive stance — no new broad index exposure. Focus on WATCH names closest to entry (MU on pullback, AMD at $192 support). NVDA at 200-day average ($178) is the highest-conviction entry point if triggered.

optimistic_case.description: Diplomatic breakthrough reopens Hormuz within 2–3 weeks; WTI pulls back to $80–85 as supply normalises; Fed signals two cuts in 2026 as energy inflation reverses; US-China tensions plateau ahead of APEC summit.
optimistic_case.market_impact: SPY rallies 5–8% as VIX compresses below 18; energy (XLE) reverses sharply; tech multiples re-rate higher; Singapore banks benefit from improved regional growth outlook.
optimistic_case.portfolio_action: If Hormuz re-opens with confirmed tanker transit data, enter NVDA at current levels and AMD near $199; reduce energy exposure focus. Singapore banks become more interesting on any rate-cut signal.

pessimistic_case.description: Iran escalates — tanker attacks increase, US naval escort plan is activated, Gulf states restrict additional supply routes; WTI breaks above $110; Fed is forced to hold rates longer as energy inflation becomes entrenched; US-China export controls expand to cover cloud software.
pessimistic_case.market_impact: SPY breaks $640 support; VIX spikes above 30; SIA (C6L_SI) faces acute margin pressure; Singapore financials re-test 2024 lows; NVDA/AMD/ASML face dual headwinds of multiple compression and supply chain disruption.
pessimistic_case.portfolio_action: Pause all new entries. If SPY breaks $640, avoid all extended names (LITE, COHR, XLE). NVDA at $169–171 support would be the first genuine BUY candidate. Cash is a valid position.

wildcard.description: US-China trade war escalates to semiconductor decoupling — China bans rare earth exports used in chip manufacturing (gallium, germanium) simultaneously with new US controls on EDA software, triggering a supply chain crisis across the entire semi stack.
wildcard.market_impact: ASML, MU, and AMD face immediate supply chain disruption; INTC (US-domestic) is the relative outperformer; gold and energy spike simultaneously as investors seek hard assets.
wildcard.portfolio_action: NVDA, AMD, ASML, and MU face sharpest forced selling in this scenario — avoid all semi entries if gallium/germanium export ban is confirmed. INTC and PLTR (defense AI, domestic) are the watchlist names most insulated.

events_this_week.event: FOMC Rate Decision — held at 3.50-3.75%; dot plot signals one 25bp cut in 2026; PCE inflation forecasts raised to 2.7%
Micron (MU) Q2 FY2026 earnings released — record revenue, blowout results; stock reaction cautious post-print per IBD
LUMENTUM (LITE) S&P 500 official inclusion effective — index fund buying pressure peaks; watch for post-inclusion reversal pattern
Post-FOMC market open — VIX at 25.09 and SPY breaking $665 support signals elevated volatility; watch for gap moves
ASML Q1 2026 earnings — key test of whether 2026 revenue guide (€34–39B) holds given US-China export control headwinds
Intel (INTC) Q1 2026 earnings — 18A node customer pipeline and foundry revenue the key metrics to watch for thesis validation
Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 2026 earnings — cloud growth rate and TPU monetisation are the two thesis-critical datapoints
DBS Group (D05_SI) earnings — net interest margin trajectory and wealth management fee growth are the key signals for the Singapore bank cluster
Amazon (AMZN) Q1 2026 earnings — Q2 operating income guide is the key test of whether the $200B capex cycle is beginning to deliver margin leverage
Palantir (PLTR) Q1 2026 earnings — government revenue growth and commercial expansion rate against the 82x forward PE are the key valuation tests
AMD Q1 2026 earnings — MI-series GPU shipment cadence and first read on the Meta supply deal are the catalysts that could change the CAUTION signal
Lumentum (LITE) Q3 FY2026 earnings — post-inclusion revenue trajectory and NVDA partnership shipment timing
Coherent (COHR) Q3 FY2026 earnings — AI datacenter revenue contribution and NVDA partnership milestones
UOB (U11_SI) earnings — NIM trend and loan growth the key metrics for Singapore bank cluster reset signal
OCBC (O39_SI) earnings — the only Singapore bank with YoY profit growth in Q4 2025; NIM and wealth management fees are the key signals

AMD MI450 + Meta 1GW first shipment milestone — per AMD IR press release, first 1GW of Instinct GPU shipments under the Meta supply deal begins H2 2026; this is the first concrete revenue event from the partnership
NVDA Blackwell Ultra (B300) volume production ramp — per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings call, next-gen architecture entering volume production Q2–Q3 2026; Vera Rubin samples shipping to customers
Intel 18A High-NA EUV ramp (Intel 14A node) — per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call and Intel investor day; H2 2026 commercial ramp is the foundry thesis validation event

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — maximum CAUTION distribution since watchlist inception
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The FOMC's hawkish hold combined with SPY breaking the $665 support level and VIX at 25.09 creates a dual headwind for every high-multiple name on the watchlist — rate-pause thesis eliminates the expansion of tech multiples precisely when the Hormuz crisis is adding inflation pressure that makes cuts even less likely.

watch_for_entry.note: Blowout Q2 validates the memory cycle but stock is 16% above 50-day average — no entry yet. Becomes actionable on a pullback toward $357–364 support zone.

on_deck.note: PEG 0.58 with 34% revenue growth — most compelling valuation on the watchlist. Samsung MOU and Meta $100B supply deal (per AMD IR) are structural catalysts.
on_deck.rationale: 18.6x forward PE at cluster median but growing nearly twice as fast as peers; 200-day average at $192.10 is next major support and potential entry zone
on_deck.trigger: Price holds $192 (200-day average) on a test with above-average volume, OR 50-day average of $214.80 flattens and price reclaims $200 on three consecutive sessions

on_deck.note: 16.4x forward PE with 73% revenue growth — 11.8% discount to semis cluster median. Blackwell Ultra ramp (per Q4 FY2026 earnings call) is the multi-quarter thesis.
on_deck.rationale: 200-day average at $178.05 is 1.3% below current price — if tested and held with volume, this is the most structurally important support level on the watchlist
on_deck.trigger: Price tests $177–179 support zone with above-average volume and RSI holds above 40 on the test

on_deck.note: PEG of 0.06 — lowest on the watchlist. NVDA $2B investment confirmed (NVIDIA IR press release). Technically blocked at +13.7% above 50-day average.
on_deck.rationale: 35x forward PE with 73% earnings growth forecast; AI datacenter structural demand is the multi-year driver; $230 support is the first re-entry zone
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $230–240 support zone with RSI dropping below 50 and volume declining on the sell-off (healthy consolidation)

caution_trim.note: Avoid — failed to hold 50-day average on FOMC day; 31.3x forward PE with only 4.9% revenue growth is expensive for near-term fundamentals. Reassess after April 15 earnings.

Avoid — 45x forward PE with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and negative free cash flow yield. 18A thesis is real but the timeline is long; no entry until April 24 earnings clarify foundry customer pipeline.
Avoid — 3.83% below declining 50-day average; PEG 2.33 is expensive for 18% revenue growth. No catalyst before April 24 earnings.
Avoid — downgraded to CAUTION today; 5.56% below declining 50-day average with FCF yield of just 1.06%. $200B capex overhang defers earnings clarity to 2027.
Avoid — hard-blocked at 33.6% extreme extension. S&P 500 inclusion buying is a known 3–5 day exhaustion pattern; wait for $528–540 support zone.
Avoid — RSI 72.6 overbought; 11% above 50-day average. Only interesting near $50–52 on a Hormuz de-escalation-driven pullback.
Avoid — 81% premium to Singapore cluster median PE with 0.9% revenue growth. Hard-blocked at 6.4% above 50-day average.
Avoid — Hormuz jet fuel cost headwind is a direct P&L risk for an airline at a 25% PE premium to the cluster. Wait for SGD 6.65 breakout with volume.
Avoid — 82x forward PE at 258% above BigTech cluster median; PEG 3.22 leaves no margin of error. Sitting on 50-day average but valuation blocks entry.
Avoid — SPY broke $665 support on FOMC day; VUAA_L 2.77% below declining 50-day average. No index exposure until 50-day average of $132.14 is reclaimed.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH above SGD 58.50 with volume. Reassess to CAUTION if NIM compression guidance worsens at April 30 earnings.
Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to SGD 21.00 (50-day average). Already 2.67% above SMA50 — no chase.
Upgrades to WATCH on sustained move above SGD 37.36 (50-day average) with volume. No action until May 7 earnings clarify NIM trajectory.
Upgrades to WATCH on confirmed break above SGD 6.65 resistance with volume. Downgrades to CAUTION if SGD 6.34 support breaks.

new_stocks_to_watch.note: TSMC is AMD's primary GPU foundry — the Meta 6GW supply deal means fab utilisation stays elevated through 2030. Monitor for watchlist addition.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8–10% from current levels toward its 50-day average

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Broadcom's silicon photonics and networking ASICs are in the same AI interconnect stack as LITE and COHR — NVDA's $4B photonics investment benefits the whole chain.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Becomes interesting if LITE/COHR pull back 15–20% from peak and AVGO corrects proportionally — monitor for entry below AVGO's 50-day average

new_stocks_to_watch.note: SK Hynix is NVDA's primary HBM supplier for Blackwell — MU's record Q2 results confirm the HBM memory cycle is real and SK Hynix benefits directly.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Revisit after MU pulls back toward $360 support — if MU confirms cycle without further extension, SK Hynix at a proportional pullback (roughly 8–12% from current KRW levels) becomes a watchlist candidate

contrarian_candidates.watching_for: Price tests $192 on above-average volume with RSI holding above 40 — if the 200-day average absorbs the sell-off without a breakdown, this is the highest-conviction entry on the watchlist

macro_trigger_map.event: FOMC Rate Decision — Held at 3.50–3.75%, hawkish dot plot
macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Powell signals two cuts in 2026 at press conference; PCE forecasts revised down in coming months
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Dot plot removes the one projected 2026 cut; Powell warns of stagflation risk from oil prices
bearish_impact: NVDA remains CAUTION — $169 support becomes key test
ASML delays further — $1,008 support becomes the realistic downside target

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Inclusion buying exhausts cleanly with no forced selling; LITE holds above $650 in the week after inclusion
bullish_upgrades: COHR → WATCH if inclusion momentum lifts the photonics pair without reversal
LITE → WATCH on pullback to $528 support if RSI drops below 50
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Post-inclusion profit-taking triggers sharp reversal below $620; SPY breakdown amplifies the selloff
bearish_impact: LITE accelerates lower — first support $528, then $317 zone
COHR follows LITE — $230 support is the key level to hold
Both photonics names remain hard CAUTION until extension compresses below 5% above SMA50

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Revenue in-line or above the €34–39B 2026 guide with strong EUV order book; China sales decline offset by European and US demand
bullish_upgrades: ASML → WATCH if price reclaims $1,382 (50-day average) on earnings day with volume
MU → reinforces memory cycle thesis if ASML confirms HBM-related lithography demand
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: China sales worse than expected; order book light for H2 2026; 2026 guide trimmed
bearish_impact: ASML remains CAUTION — $1,008 support is the structural floor
Semis cluster broadly pressured — NVDA, AMD face additional headwinds from tool availability uncertainty
